# dragonlight-router

> Multi-provider LLM routing engine — intelligent model selection and cascade dispatch across 11 providers.

## Overview

dragonlight-router selects the best available model for each request, dispatches through a three-stage cascade (MBR → CBR → LBR), and handles fallback automatically when providers fail. It replaces hand-rolled provider switching, health-check spreadsheets, and rate-limit math with a single component.

## Key Features

- 11 providers: Anthropic, Cerebras, Cohere, Gemini, Groq, Mistral, NVIDIA NIM, Ollama, OpenAI, OpenRouter, Together
- MBR → CBR → LBR cascade: capability filtering, cost-aware scoring, rate-limit enforcement
- Circuit breaking: failing models removed from rotation until recovery
- Budget enforcement: sliding-window RPM, RPD, and TPM tracking per provider
- Dual interface: ranked model list (you call the LLM) or full dispatch with fallback
- Hot-reload: change role-to-model matrix or provider config without restart
- Response caching: exact-match and semantic near-duplicate caching (SQLite-backed)
- SSE streaming: token-by-token events with automatic fallback

## Quick Start

```bash
pip install -e ".[all]"
```

```python
from dragonlight_router import RouterEngine

router = RouterEngine()
models = router.select_models("code_review", top_n=5)
```

## Documentation

https://dragonlightintl.github.io/dragonlight-router/
